opportunity.statement: ∀ {Γ Δ Λ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, (Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A)) → (Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C) → (Δ ++ Γ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ C)
opportunity.unlocks: It completes the missing slash-to-context transport bridge by giving the right-division half of arbitrary ambient-context replacement, directly addressing the bottleneck that there is still no metatheorem turning both slash derivations into global proof transformations.
opportunity.why_now: The proof of `op_000005` made the mechanism explicit: invertibility of a slash derivation plus `cut_admissible` yields a context-replacement theorem. Since `rdiv_invertible` is already available, the solve event exposes an immediate reusable template for the right-division case rather than a fresh search problem.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not just a symmetric nearby corollary because the current frontier explicitly lacks a general two-sided contextual residuation bridge. Adding only the left-division theorem leaves the main metatheorem incomplete; proving the right-division counterpart upgrades the theory from a one-off transport fact to a reusable slash-level context transformation principle.

opportunity.statement: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ ∀ {Δ Λ : List Tp} {C : Tp}, Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C → Δ ++ Γ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ C
opportunity.unlocks: An exact metatheorem for right division: it would close the bottleneck of explaining when a local slash derivation is precisely the same as a reusable ambient-context transport rule, and would give a template for the left-division criterion as well.
opportunity.why_now: The hard direction became available only after the solve event: the proved contextual replacement theorem gives the universal transport half, while the converse is now visible by instantiating the transport rule with `[B] ⇒ B` and then applying `rdiv_r` using the explicit `Γ ≠ []` side condition.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not a nearby corollary or another symmetric closure variant. It upgrades the new theorem into a sharp iff-characterization of right division itself, turning an admissible rule into an exact criterion for derivability and materially changing the theory summary.

opportunity.statement: ∀ {Γ Δ Λ : List Tp} {A B C : Tp}, Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) → Δ ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ C → Δ ++ Γ ++ [A] ++ Λ ⇒ C
opportunity.unlocks: A verified right-division contextual replacement theorem, directly addressing the missing bridge from slash derivations to ambient-context transformations and advancing the contextual_residuation_bridges agenda.
opportunity.why_now: The solved theorem made the key local transport step explicit: from Γ ⇒ (B ⧸ A) and a proof of A, one can derive B in the appended context. That turns right-division provability into a cut-ready replacement mechanism, so the jump from local application to arbitrary-context substitution is now concrete rather than speculative.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not a nearby corollary of the same shape. It upgrades a local append application lemma into a reusable metatheorem for rewriting larger sequents, exactly matching a stated bottleneck and producing summary-level proof transport rather than another one-off slash composition fact.

opportunity.statement: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A B : Tp}, (Γ ⇒ (A ⧹ B)) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ ∀ {Δ : List Tp}, Δ ⇒ A → Δ ++ Γ ⇒ B
opportunity.unlocks: an exact residuation-style reduction principle for left division, which can then be paired with the right-division side into a higher-level substitution/reducibility package rather than more isolated slash lemmas
opportunity.why_now: Before this solve, the theory only had the one-point invertibility view `[A] ++ Γ ⇒ B`; the new application theorem upgrades that to arbitrary derivable arguments `Δ ⇒ A`, so the forward half of a genuine extensional criterion is now available. The reverse half is then immediate from `ldiv_r` once the real obstruction `Γ ≠ []` is stated explicitly.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not just a nearby corollary of the solved lemma: it isolates the exact boundary condition for left division (nonempty antecedent) and turns a local application fact into an iff characterization of what `A ⧹ B` means in the sequent calculus. That materially sharpens the current transport backbone into a reusable metatheorem.

opportunity.statement: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → (Γ ⇒ A ↔ ∃ s : String, Γ = [#s] ∧ [#s] ⇒ A)
opportunity.unlocks: A sharp restricted-regime classification: atom-only derivability reduces exactly to the singleton-atom source case, which would fill the current missing boundary theorem and set up a second-stage study of which targets are derivable from one atom.
opportunity.why_now: The counterexample shows the failed theorem breaks on the succedent side, not on the context side: the bad derivation already uses Γ = [#a]. That makes the surviving invariant visible: under an atom-only hypothesis, the real constraint is that any derivable context must collapse to a singleton atom, while the succedent may still be composite.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not a routine weakening of the false statement. It isolates the exact criterion that survives the refutation, directly addresses a listed bottleneck about restricted-regime boundaries, and cleanly separates a genuine boundary phenomenon from the disproved stronger claim.
